notes: Petition-sourced discoveries from Change.org. Each entry was cross-referenced with news articles for additional details on companies, capacity, investment, and opposition groups. Everett MA petition exists but no specific developer or project could be identified from news sources - it appears to be a preemptive ban petition.

new_entries.summary: Change.org petition with 5,856 signatures opposing a proposed data center on Robertsville Road in Villa Ridge, near Shaw Nature Reserve. The land (613 acres total) is owned by Diamond 66 LLC and Diamond Farms LLC; L.B. Eckelkamp Jr., CEO of Bank of Washington, requested Franklin County rezone the parcels. Franklin County is under a drought alert. After hours of public comment, the Planning and Zoning Commission voted to table the discussion for six months. The petition was started by Amanda Brown and targets Governor Kehoe.
new_entries.company: Diamond Farms LLC / L.B. Eckelkamp Jr.

new_entries.summary: Change.org petition with 1,968 signatures opposing mega data centers in Hidalgo County and the Rio Grande Valley. The primary project driving concern is Eneus Energy's RGV Data Center near Harlingen (Cameron County), which plans up to 16 data center halls requiring 1 GW of electricity and 4.6 million gallons/day of reclaimed wastewater. Eneus projects $14 billion in capital investment and 1,000 jobs. Petition started by Joshua Moroles. South Texas city officials say they have limited power to intervene since sites are outside city boundaries. Harlingen is drafting regulations in response.

Change.org petition with 1,052 signatures opposing TeraWulf's data center at the former Century Aluminum smelter site in Hawesville. TeraWulf (via Justified DataPower LLC) purchased the 750-acre site for $200 million and plans to invest $3-4 billion to build a 'clean digital infrastructure' campus with 480 MW of existing power availability. Phase 1 targeted for late 2027. The Kentucky Conservation Committee (led by Lane Boldman) has voiced opposition, calling it a 'missed opportunity for clean aluminum manufacturing.' Kentucky House Bill 544 (Ratepayer Protection Act) was proposed in response. Community concerns include electricity rate increases and limited permanent employment (~100 jobs).

Change.org petition with 650 signatures calling for a ban on data center construction in Everett, MA. Started by Stephanie McColaugh on Feb 24, 2026 and gaining signatures rapidly (453/week). The petition appears to be preemptive rather than targeting a specific announced project, citing concerns about utility rate increases, water depletion, and air/noise pollution. No specific developer or project has been publicly announced for Everett. Massachusetts enacted a data center sales tax exemption in November 2024 and a $3 billion hyperscale facility has been proposed in Westfield.

Change.org petition with 395 signatures calling to ban new data centers on farmland and in towns, focused on protecting the Driftless Area. An undisclosed company (described as 'one of the big five or six') is seeking 400-500 acres in Cassville for a $1 billion data center. The site is along the Cardinal-Hickory Transmission Line. A 'No Data Centers in the Driftless' movement has formed, led by local volunteers including Pete Moris (Mount Hope) and Melodie Betts (Fennimore). A community meeting held at the Lemon Door restaurant in Fennimore and a larger event scheduled March 8 at Holiday Gardens in Potosi. Residents protested at the Grant County Courthouse. The developer has been vague about power consumption specifics.
opposition_groups: No Data Centers in the Driftless

new_entries.summary: Change.org petition with 216 signatures opposing data centers in Temple. Rowan Digital Infrastructure received unanimous city council approval for a $700 million, 300 MW hyperscale data center campus on 700 acres along Bob White Road. Construction started fall 2025 with 12-18 month timeline. The facility borders Robinson Family Farm. Rowan says it will use a closed-loop cooling system with minimal water consumption (1-2 million gallons filled once, lasting 10-15 years). Meta also previously paused an $800 million data center project in Temple in 2022. Community concerns center on energy consumption and infrastructure impact.

Change.org petition with 180 signatures titled 'No 3-Year Moratorium? No Vote' demanding Pennsylvania enact a statewide data center moratorium. 1778 Rich Pike LLC / Quantm Group plans 'Project Gouldsboro,' a massive data center campus of up to 35 buildings across ~1,000 acres in Clifton and Covington Townships requiring 1,500 MW of power and $80 million in PPL grid upgrades. Covington supervisors passed a 3-2 zoning amendment (DCET zone) after a closed-door session despite strong opposition. About 3,000 residents signed a separate community petition (twice the township's population). Clifton Township's settlement with the developer was later denied by a judge. The No Data Center Clifton/Covington TWPs group and Save Pennsylvania (NCDC) are leading organized opposition with support from PennFuture and Pocono Heritage Land Trust.

Change.org petition with 67 signatures opposing data center construction in Commercial Point and Scioto Township. The Commercial Point Data Campus is a proposed 152-acre facility marketed by JLL, featuring 1.75 million square feet of space with an initial 75,000 sq ft expanding to 220,000 sq ft. It includes a 1.3 GW natural gas plant and solar array, with 450 MW of grid-connected power planned by 2029. Not affiliated with nearby AWS facilities. Pickaway County Commissioners voted unanimously to deny a 344-acre annexation petition in April 2025. Teays Valley Against Overgrowth (TVAO), founded by Cory Wasmus, is the primary opposition group, citing traffic, noise, and environmental impacts from fossil fuel reliance.
